abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit callout engine.

inlineContent.text: Callouts are an important part of the typing experience, where input callouts can show the currently pressed key and action callouts can show secondary actions when certain keys are long pressed.
KeyboardKit can show an
for the currently pressed input key, and an
with secondary actions when a key with secondary actions is long pressed.
unlocks localized callouts for all
locales in your license. Information about Pro features can be found further down.
KeyboardKit has a
namespace that contains callout-related types and views. For instance, an
can show the currently pressed character, and an
can show secondary actions.
KeyboardKit has an observable
class that provides observable input and action callout state, such as the currently pressed
or the
to present.
KeyboardKit automatically creates an instance of this class and injects it into
, then updates this context when keys are pressed.
In KeyboardKit, a
can return secondary callout actions when a key is long pressed and trigger feedback when the selected secondary action changes.

KeyboardKit 9.5 introduced a new action builder-based
view modifier that will replace the entire service concept in the next major version, if it proves to be easier to use than services.
You can use
view modifier to customize callout actions for any action, instead of using a service:

code: // See the demo app for a better, interactive example.
... // Insert the view to use here, for instance a KeyboardView

inlineContent.text: You can customize callouts for any actions, then use
to return the standard actions for all other actions.
KeyboardKit will use the callout service if this modifier returns nil value and view modifier-based model proves better, since using values removes many complexities that are involved with services.
You can use the
view modifier to apply a custom callout style, or implement and register a custom
. This style will be used by both the action callout and the input callout.
The
namespace has callout-specific views, that can be used to mimic the native iOS input and action callouts.
The
view can present secondary actions when a keyboard key with secondary actions is long-pressed.
The
view can be used to zoom in the currently pressed input key as the user types.

KeyboardKit Pro also unlocks localized callout
for all supported locales that are included in your license.

The services and value builders will throw a license error if you try to access them without a valid KeyboardKit Pro license. If you are on the Basic or Silver plan, you must specify which locales to use in your

The localized callout action values are static values that will be lazily computed when you first access them. They will fail silently if you try to access them before registering a valid license, which will cause them to always have a
value.

will automatically make both input and action callouts work. For a custom view, just apply a
view modifier to it to make it present callouts.
